idea_candidates.name: Jump-to-Line Feature
idea_candidates.summary: Implement a 'Jump-to-Line' feature allowing users to quickly navigate to a specific line number in the inventory list.
idea_candidates.proposed_solution: Add a search bar or input field where users can enter a line number to jump directly to that position in the list.
idea_candidates.value_proposition: Saves time and reduces frustration by eliminating the need to scroll through long lists manually.
idea_candidates.differentiation: Unlike basic scrolling, this feature provides precise and instant navigation to any part of the list.
idea_candidates.revenue_model: Improved user efficiency leads to higher productivity and potential cost savings for businesses.
idea_candidates.feasibility: Technically feasible with minimal development effort, as it involves adding a simple input field and navigation logic.
risks: Potential usability issues if the feature is not intuitive

idea_candidates.name: Incremental Scroll Buttons
idea_candidates.summary: Add buttons to navigate by specific increments (e.g., 5 or 10 lines) within inventory lists.
idea_candidates.proposed_solution: Include buttons labeled 'Scroll +5' and 'Scroll +10' next to the list for quick navigation.
idea_candidates.value_proposition: Enhances navigation speed and reduces the effort required to move through large lists.
idea_candidates.differentiation: Provides a more structured and controlled way to navigate compared to traditional scrolling.
idea_candidates.revenue_model: Increased user satisfaction and efficiency contribute to better operational outcomes.
idea_candidates.feasibility: Straightforward to implement with existing UI frameworks and minimal backend changes.
risks: Overcrowding of the UI if not designed carefully
